public_functions.behavior_description: Enable and clock the eGTM module. Configure the eGTM CMU to provide the ATOM submodule clock from the selected FXCLK source and set the DTM/CDTM clock source as required. Initialize the unified PWM configuration with defaults, select the ATOM submodule and assign three contiguous channels (base + 2 sync) for a 3-phase inverter. Set center-aligned mode with a 20 kHz frequency on the base channel and enable synchronized start/update across all configured channels. For each of the three channels, assign an output configuration that drives complementary outputs via the dead-time module (CDTM) with active-high polarity for the main output and active-low for the complementary output; configure the pads in push-pull mode with appropriate pad drive strength. Configure deadtime for both rising and falling edges to 1 microsecond using the DTM/CDTM configuration (and/or apply an immediate deadtime update after init). Set up a single period-event interrupt on CPU0 with the requested priority, attach it to the first (base) channel, and leave other channels without interrupts. Initialize the PWM driver with the prepared config and per-channel configurations, update global control/unit data if required by the driver, install the ISR handler and initialize interrupt routing, then start all channels synchronously so they run in lockstep.

Maintain an array of three duty cycle values, one per phase. On each call: for each phase, check whether adding a fixed STEP value would reach or exceed 100%. If so, wrap that phase's duty to 0.0 before applying the increment. Then increment all three phase duties by STEP. After updating all three values, apply them immediately to the PWM driver using the multi-channel immediate update API so that the new duty cycles take effect coherently across all configured channels while preserving center alignment and configured deadtime.
